siteSettings.name: Next Steps
siteSettings.tagline: Helping families navigate what comes after high school

hero.headline: Life after high school shouldn't feel like guesswork
hero.subheadline: Next Steps helps parents and teens explore college, military, work, trade school, apprenticeships, and entrepreneurship together — with a plan, not just pressure.
hero.imageAlt: Parent and teenager reviewing options together at a table

sections.heading: Every path forward deserves a real conversation
sections.body: A lot of teens don't have someone they trust to talk through what's next — and a lot of parents don't know where to start. Next Steps gives families a structured, judgment-free way to explore every option: college, military, workforce, trade school, apprenticeship, or starting a business.

sections.heading: How We Help
sections.subheading: One-on-one guidance and group workshops built around real options, not one path

sections.heading: Four Tracks. One Goal: Clarity.
sections.subheading: Our workshops are organized around the four most common next-step paths

sections.heading: Ready to help your teen find their next step?
sections.body: Book a consultation and let's map out the options together.

seo.title: Next Steps — College, Military, Trade & Career Planning for Teens | Calera, AL
seo.description: Next Steps helps parents and teens in Calera, AL explore every path after high school — college, military, workforce, trade school, and entrepreneurship — through 1-on-1 consultations and group workshops.

hero.headline: Built by a mom who wished this existed
hero.subheadline: Next Steps started with one family's search for better information — now it's here for yours.

sections.heading: Our story
sections.body: It all started in 2022, when founder Bessie Miller was preparing her own daughter for the transition out of high school. As a mother and public service worker, Bessie found herself wishing there was a service like this — one place to help families make informed, confident decisions about what comes next, without pushing any single path. Next Steps launched in 2026 to be exactly that resource for other families in Calera and beyond.

items.title: Transparency
items.description: We lay out every option honestly, including the tradeoffs, so families can make informed decisions.

items.title: Customer oriented
items.description: Every family's situation is different. We build the plan around your teen, not a template.

items.title: Data driven
items.description: Our guidance is grounded in real information about each path, not assumptions or trends.

seo.title: About Next Steps | Founder Bessie Miller
seo.description: Next Steps was founded in 2022 by Bessie Miller, a mother and public service worker, to help families navigate every option after high school with transparency and real data.

hero.headline: Guidance built around your teen's path
hero.subheadline: Whether it's one focused conversation or a full workshop series, we meet families where they are.

hero.headline: Track-based resources for every next-step path
hero.subheadline: Explore curated resources across College, Workforce, Trade, and Military tracks. We offer a small set of free starting points and deeper paid resources behind a member paywall.

sections.heading: Start free, then go deeper
sections.body: Each track includes one free resource to help families get started fast. Most resources are premium and include deeper templates, guided checklists, and planning tools unlocked through paid access.

sections.heading: Need access to paid resources?
sections.body: Choose your track and contact us for membership options and current pricing.

hero.subheadline: Book a consultation or send us a message — we respond within 1 business day.

options.label: Book a consultation
options.description: Pick a time that works for your family

seo.title: Contact Next Steps | Book a Consultation
seo.description: Reach out to Next Steps to book a 1-on-1 consultation or ask about workshop tracks in Calera, AL.

services.name: 1-on-1 Family Consultation
services.shortDescription: Meet with our team to map out career paths and options for your child.
services.fullDescription: A focused, private session for your family to explore every realistic option after high school — college, military, workforce, trade school, apprenticeship, or entrepreneurship — and leave with a concrete plan tailored to your teen.
services.approach: We start with where your teen is today: interests, strengths, constraints, and concerns. From there we walk through each path honestly, including cost, timeline, and tradeoffs, before narrowing down to a realistic short list.
services.whoItsFor: Parents and teens (15+) who want personalized guidance rather than generic advice.
outcomes: An honest look at options the family may not have considered
Reduced stress and better communication between parent and teen
services.imageAlt: Advisor meeting with a parent and teenager

services.name: Group Workshops
services.shortDescription: Track-specific group workshops covering College, Workforce, Trade, and Military paths.
services.fullDescription: Our workshops bring families together to explore a specific path in depth, guided by real information and structured activities. Choose one track or attend multiple to compare paths side by side.
services.approach: Each workshop is organized around one of four tracks and combines short presentations, discussion, and planning exercises so families leave with next steps, not just information.
services.whoItsFor: Parents and teens who want a deeper dive into a specific path, or who want to compare paths in a group setting.
outcomes: In-depth understanding of one or more paths
Connection with other families navigating the same decisions
services.imageAlt: Group of parents and teens at a workshop table

workshopTracks.name: College Track
workshopTracks.tagline: Turn applications and affordability into a real decision.
workshopTracks.description: Covers applications, financial aid, choosing a major, and campus fit.
workshopTracks.fullDescription: This track helps families make sense of the college path from both the student and parent side. We break down application timelines, compare school-fit factors, and talk honestly about cost, debt, scholarships, and what a degree path actually requires once classes begin.
workshopTracks.audience: Best for students considering two-year or four-year colleges and families who want a clearer process for narrowing options without relying on guesswork.

topics.title: Admissions game plan
topics.description: Map out application timing, testing expectations, recommendation needs, and decision deadlines.

topics.title: Cost and financial aid
topics.description: Compare tuition, scholarships, FAFSA basics, and the long-term impact of borrowing.

topics.title: School fit
topics.description: Evaluate majors, campus culture, support services, and distance from home with more clarity.

outcomes.title: Short list of options
outcomes.description: Families leave with a clearer college list based on fit, budget, and goals.

outcomes.title: Application next steps
outcomes.description: Students know what to do next and when to do it after the workshop ends.

outcomes.title: Better financial questions
outcomes.description: Parents leave knowing which cost questions to ask before saying yes to a school.

workshopTracks.name: Workforce Track
workshopTracks.tagline: Build a launch plan for work right after high school.
workshopTracks.description: Covers entry-level job search, resumes, interview skills, and building a career without a degree.
workshopTracks.fullDescription: This track is for students who want to move directly into the workforce and families who want that decision treated seriously. We cover job-search basics, early career planning, employer expectations, and how to turn an entry-level role into a pathway instead of a dead end.
workshopTracks.audience: Best for students exploring immediate employment, gap-year work experience, or non-degree career paths that still need structure and momentum.

topics.title: Resume and job search basics
topics.description: Learn how to present school, volunteer, and part-time experience in a way employers understand.

topics.title: Interview readiness
topics.description: Practice how to answer common questions, communicate reliability, and follow up professionally.

topics.title: Career growth planning
topics.description: Look at certifications, advancement steps, and how to choose first jobs with future options in mind.

outcomes.title: First-job checklist
outcomes.description: Students leave with a concrete set of steps for applying, interviewing, and following through.

outcomes.title: Professional communication tools
outcomes.description: Families gain scripts and expectations for interviews, email follow-up, and workplace professionalism.

outcomes.title: Longer-term pathway thinking
outcomes.description: Students can connect a first role to advancement, training, and earning potential over time.

workshopTracks.imageAlt: Young adult in a workplace setting

workshopTracks.name: Trade Track
workshopTracks.tagline: Compare skilled-trade options with less confusion and more facts.
workshopTracks.description: Covers trade school options, certifications, apprenticeship pathways, and skilled-trade careers.
workshopTracks.fullDescription: This track gives families a realistic overview of skilled-trade routes, from trade schools to apprenticeship programs. We compare training models, entry requirements, wages, physical demands, and what different trades can offer over time so students can make an informed choice.
workshopTracks.audience: Best for students interested in hands-on careers, paid training paths, or certification-based work in high-demand industries.

topics.title: Trade pathways
topics.description: Understand the differences between trade school, certification programs, unions, and apprenticeships.

topics.title: Career and wage outlook
topics.description: Review what various trades pay, how advancement works, and what day-to-day work can look like.

topics.title: Readiness and fit
topics.description: Talk through transportation, equipment, physical expectations, and commitment level before choosing a path.

outcomes.title: Path comparison worksheet
outcomes.description: Families can compare several trade routes side by side instead of relying on hearsay.

outcomes.title: Questions for programs and employers
outcomes.description: Students leave knowing what to ask when evaluating schools, training programs, and apprenticeships.

outcomes.title: Actionable next moves
outcomes.description: Each attendee leaves with a short list of programs, certifications, or contacts to pursue next.

workshopTracks.imageAlt: Student in a trade or vocational training setting

workshopTracks.name: Military Track
workshopTracks.tagline: Understand service options, structure, and tradeoffs before committing.
workshopTracks.description: Covers branches, enlistment process, ASVAB prep, and life planning around military service.
workshopTracks.fullDescription: This track helps students and parents evaluate military service with more clarity and fewer assumptions. We walk through branch differences, enlistment basics, ASVAB preparation, benefits, obligations, and the practical questions families should ask before a student signs anything.
workshopTracks.audience: Best for students considering enlistment, ROTC-adjacent planning, or military service as part of a broader post-high-school strategy.

topics.title: Branch overview
topics.description: Compare mission, lifestyle, culture, and common entry expectations across branches.

topics.title: Enlistment process
topics.description: Understand the recruiting process, required documents, timing, and major decision points.

topics.title: Benefits and commitments
topics.description: Talk through education benefits, career training, relocation, and the realities of service commitments.

outcomes.title: Clearer decision framework
outcomes.description: Families leave with the right questions to evaluate service as a serious option, not a vague idea.

outcomes.title: Preparation steps
outcomes.description: Students understand what to do next for ASVAB prep, recruiter conversations, and documentation.

outcomes.title: Balanced expectations
outcomes.description: Parents and teens gain a more honest view of the benefits, structure, and sacrifices involved.

workshopTracks.imageAlt: Family talking with a military recruiter or advisor

resources.description: A decision worksheet to compare education benefits, service commitments, and lifestyle tradeoffs.
